summary: Research the Qwen3.5/3.6 HYBRID (Gated-DeltaNet linear-attn + periodic full-attn + MTP) forward pass for sm_120: exact kernels from llama.cpp/FLA/vLLM/SGLang, to build a logit-matched engine

result.playbook:
Wrote `/home/avifenesh/projects/bw24/PHASE1-HYBRID.md` — a concrete, copy-oriented Phase-1 playbook grounded in the real repo (existing `bw24-gguf` crate, `ModelConfig`/`LayerKind`, dequant oracle, `bw24-probe` sm_120a fatbin spine) and verified llama.cpp line numbers.

Key facts I corrected/confirmed against the actual sources while writing it:
- `qwen35.cpp:25` confirms the recurrent-layer rule `(i+1)%full_attention_interval!=0`, and `config.rs:156-165` already implements `layer_kind()` identically — the doc tells the implementer to reuse it, not re-derive it.
- `gated_delta_net.cu:84-111` (decode !KDA branch), `:54` transposed state `M[col][i]`, `:283` scale=1/sqrt(S_v), launch grid `:184-185`, and the `//TODO chunked kernel` at `:181` all match the research verbatim.
- `ssm-conv.cu:5-58` (n_t<=32 register path) / `:60-124` (long-token smem) / `:160-206` op dispatch confirmed; conv weight is kernel-major and SiLU is fused via `apply_silu`.
- `qwen35.cpp:269-296` fused per-head q|gate split (stride 512, gate at offset 256), `:302-312` IMRoPE, `:326-329` `attn*sigmoid(gate)` after SDPA, `:472-485` dense SwiGLU with `ffn_gate_inp==nullptr` assert.

The doc delivers all 7 required pieces: (1) module layout for a new `crates/bw24-engine`, (2) exact per-layer-type forward graphs with shapes and llama.cpp line cites, (3) dual-cache design with the 24GB math (32 KiB/token KV growing only on 8/16 full-attn layers vs constant ~52.7 MiB recurrent state), (4) the Stage-1 correctness-first / Stage-2 sm_120-fast kernel table, (5) per-kernel copy targets across llama.cpp/FLA/vLLM, (6) the logit-match harness with 1e-2 abs / 1e-3 rel tolerance and a bisect order, and (7) an ordered milestone list starting at M0 = pure-dense Qwen3-1.7B spine proof through M9 = Stage-2 fast path.

One note for the parent: the smallest-milestone suggestion (M0) targets Qwen3-1.7B, but only the 9B Q8_0 GGUF is present locally at `/home/avifenesh/ai-ml/models/`; a small dense Qwen3 GGUF would need to be fetched to run M0 as written.

components.component: Gated DeltaNet linear-attention scan (the 24 recurrent layers of Qwen3.5-9B / Qwen3.6-27B hybrid). The exact per-layer math, prefill-chunk vs decode-recurrent designs, and sm_120 kernel plan to port.
components.math:
SHAPES (n_embd=4096; from qwen35.cpp build_layer_attn_linear lines 344-349 — note dim names are NON-OBVIOUS):
- ssm_d_state=128, ssm_n_group=16, ssm_dt_rank=32, ssm_d_inner=4096, ssm_d_conv=4.
- num_k_heads = ssm_n_group = 16; num_v_heads = ssm_dt_rank = 32; head_k_dim = head_v_dim = ssm_d_state = 128 ( = d_inner/num_v_heads = 4096/32). So S (state dim) = 128 for BOTH k and v. GVA: 32 v-heads / 16 k-heads => repeat each k/q head 2x.
- key_dim = head_k_dim*num_k_heads = 128*16 = 2048. value_dim = head_v_dim*num_v_heads = 128*32 = 4096. conv_channels (conv_dim) = key_dim*2 + value_dim = 2048*2+4096 = 8192 = d_inner + 2*n_group*d_state.
- State matrix per (seq, v-head): S in R^{128x128} (S_k x S_v). Total recurrent state per layer = num_v_heads * 128 * 128 = 32*16384 = 524288 floats/seq = 2MB f32/seq. conv state = 8192*(4-1)=24576 floats/seq.

PROJECTIONS (qwen35.cpp build_qkvz lines 230-244 + build_layer_attn_linear 357-470):
- wqkv [4096, 2048*2+4096=8192] -> qkv_mixed (q|k|v concatenated along feature). wqkv_gate (attn_gate) [4096, 4096] -> z (output gate).
- beta = sigmoid( ssm_beta[4096,32] @ x ), reshape [1, 32(=num_v_heads), T, n_seqs]  (lines 361-366).
- alpha = ssm_alpha[4096,32] @ x; a_softplus = softplus(alpha + ssm_dt.bias[32]); gate g_raw = a_softplus * ssm_a[32]  where ssm_a holds -exp(A_log) (comment line 376 "-A_log.exp() * softplus"). So g_log = -exp(A_log)*softplus(alpha+dt_bias), reshape [1,32,T,n_seqs]. (qwen35 uses SEPARATE ssm_beta & ssm_alpha tensors; qwen3next.cpp fuses them into one ssm_beta_alpha then splits — same result.)
- NOTE: qwen35.cpp passes RAW g_log into the op (it is in log space); the CUDA kernel does expf(g) per step. beta is pre-sigmoid'd in the graph. This differs from FLA use_gate_in_kernel=True path.

CONV (build_conv_state in delta-net-base.cpp 449-525 + ggml_ssm_conv): qkv_mixed (8192 ch) concat with prev conv_state (kernel-1=3 cols) along time, depthwise causal conv1d kernel=4 (ssm_conv1d [4,8192]), then SiLU. Then split conv output into q(128x16), k(128x16), v(128x32) by channel offsets 0 / 2048 / 4096 (lines 407-423). q,k get ggml_l2_norm (eps=1e-6) along head_dim (lines 431-432). If num_k_heads!=num_v_heads and not fused: repeat-interleave q,k to 32 heads.

CORE RECURRENCE (per token t, per v-head h, state S in R^{128(k) x 128(v)}). VERIFIED IDENTICAL across 4 sources:
  g_t = exp(g_log_t)        # scalar per head, in (0,1]
  beta_t = sigmoid(...)     # scalar per head (already applied in graph)
  q~,k~ = l2norm(q),l2norm(k)
  kv   = (g_t * S)^T k~     # what state predicts:  kv[v] = sum_k g_t*S[k][v]*k~[k]
  delta= beta_t * (v - kv)  # error-corrected value, R^128
  S    = g_t*S + k~ (outer) delta     # S[k][v] = g_t*S[k][v] + k~[k]*delta[v]
  o_t  = S^T q~  * scale    # o[v] = sum_k S[k][v]*q~[k];  scale = 1/sqrt(S_k)=1/sqrt(128)
This is the FLA fused_recurrent_gated_delta_rule_fwd_kernel inner loop (state_v_first=False branch) AND the llama.cpp gated_delta_net_cuda<S_v,KDA=false> inner loop (lines 84-111). KDA branch (g per-channel, ssm_d_state-wide) is for Kimi-Linear; Qwen3.5 uses GDA scalar gate (g->ne[0]==1) so KDA=false.

POST: gated RMSNorm (build_norm_gated 246-255): out = RMSNorm(o, ssm_norm[128]) * silu(z); reshape to [value_dim=4096, T, n_seqs]; ssm_out [4096,4096] projects back to n_embd=4096.

CHUNKED PREFILL MATH (delta-net-base.cpp build_delta_net_chunking 16-287, CS=64 for GDA): g_cs = within-chunk cumsum of g_log. decay_mask[i,j]=exp(g_cs[i]-g_cs[j]) lower-tri. WY: solve (I+tril(diag-excl k_beta@k^T*L)) to get T-matrix; v_new = T@(v*beta) - T@(k_cumdecay@S_prev); intra = (q@k^T*L_tri)@v_new; inter = (q*exp(g_cs))@S_prev; o=inter+intra. Cross-chunk: S = S*exp(g_last) + (k*exp(g_last-g_cs))^T @ v_new.
components.chosen_design:
TWO kernels, staged correct-first then fast:

DECODE (single token, n_tokens==1) — RECURRENT, THE priority path: port the llama.cpp gated_delta_net_cuda<S_v=128, KDA=false, keep_rs_t=false> kernel verbatim. Grid (H=32, n_seqs, ceil(S_v/4)); block (warp_size<=128?warp:128, 4 warps). Each warp owns one v-column 'col' of the 128x128 state; rows sharded across lanes (rows_per_lane=128/32=4). Per token: warp_reduce_sum over k~ to get kv[col]; delta[col]=(v[col]-g*kv[col])*beta; fused S[i][col]=g*S[i][col]+k~[i]*delta[col] AND attn[col]=sum_i S[i][col]*q~[i]; final attn*scale. State kept in registers across the (here single) token. This is exact, fast, and needs NO tensor cores — pure CUDA + warp shuffles. Maps cleanly to sm_120.

PREFILL (n_tokens>1) — start with the SAME recurrent kernel (loop over tokens, state in registers) for a CORRECT baseline; it is O(T) sequential but trivially correct. Then optimize to the chunked WY form (build_delta_net_chunking) which is a sequence of batched mul_mat (chunk x chunk = 64x64, and 128x64) + tri/solve_tri/cumsum/exp — these are GEMM-shaped and map to sm_120 mma.sync m16n8k16 (FP16/TF32 verified working). The llama.cpp comment at gated_delta_net.cu:181 confirms a chunked kernel is the planned fast prefill path (not yet written there). For sm_120 prefill, copy FLA's chunk decomposition (chunk_gated_delta_rule_fwd: gate cumsum -> WY repr via solve_tril -> chunk_gated_delta_rule_fwd_h state recurrence over chunks -> chunk_fwd_o), implementing each matmul as mma.sync tiles.

components.sm120_kernel_plan:
STAGE 1 (CORRECT, both prefill+decode, no tensor cores): port gated_delta_net.cu's gated_delta_net_cuda<128,false,false> as a plain CUDA kernel. F32 state, warp = one v-column of the 128x128 state, 4 warps/block, rows_per_lane=4. Inner loop = warp_reduce_sum (use __shfl_down_sync, S_v=128 spans 4 warps along columns but each reduction is within a 32-lane warp over the 128 rows sharded 4/lane — exactly as written). expf for gate, scale=1/sqrt(128)=0.0883883. For prefill just iterate t=0..n_tokens-1 in the same kernel (state stays in registers) — O(T) but logit-exact. This compiles to sm_120a with plain nvcc, NO wgmma/tcgen05/mma needed. Feed it q,k,v already conv'd + L2-normed + g_log (raw, kernel does exp) + beta (pre-sigmoid'd) + s0 state, matching the ggml op's src contract. Validate against build_delta_net_autoregressive on CPU and against llama.cpp CUDA output token-by-token.
Supporting kernels Stage 1: (a) depthwise causal conv1d+SiLU — port ssm_conv.cu directly (it is plain f32, no TC). (b) l2_norm along head_dim=128 (trivial warp reduce). (c) RMSNorm(128)*silu(z) gated norm. (d) plain GEMMs for wqkv/wqkv_gate/ssm_beta/ssm_alpha/ssm_out via existing engine matmul.
STAGE 2 (FAST prefill): implement chunked WY form. Tiles are 64x64 (CS) and 128x64 — all GEMM-shaped, target sm_120 mma.sync m16n8k16 with FP16/TF32 accumulate-in-F32 (VERIFIED to run on this box). Pipeline per delta-net-base.cpp build_delta_net_chunking: g cumsum (scan, small), decay_mask exp, kkt = k@k_b^T scaled by L, solve_tri (forward-substitution; do as small per-chunk triangular solve in shared mem — sequential over 64 rows, cheap), v_new, then sequential cross-chunk state recurrence (n_chunks steps, each a 128x64 @ 64x128 GEMM) + intra/inter outputs. Keep decode kernel for n_tokens==1. The cross-chunk loop is the only sequential part and is short (n_chunks = ceil(T/64)).
STAGE 2 alt for decode batching/MTP speculative: support keep_rs_t=true (K>1 snapshot slots) path if MTP rollback is wanted, mirroring gated_delta_net.cu lines 145-158 (writes K most-recent states into output).
KDA branch (per-channel gate) is NOT needed for Qwen3.5 (GDA scalar gate). Skip it; only build the g->ne[0]==1 path.
pitfalls: DIM NAMING TRAP: ssm_n_group(16)=num_K_heads, ssm_dt_rank(32)=num_V_heads, ssm_d_state(128)=head_dim for BOTH k and v. The prompt's 'key_dim=state_size*group_count, value_dim=state_size*time_step_rank' is RIGHT (2048 and 4096) but easy to invert. head_v_dim is computed as d_inner/num_v_heads = 4096/32 = 128, which equals ssm_d_state by construction — do not assume head_v_dim independent.

GATE IS PASSED IN LOG SPACE: qwen35.cpp computes g_log = -exp(A_log)*softplus(alpha+dt_bias) and passes it RAW to the op; the CUDA kernel applies expf(g) internally (line 85). Do NOT exp it twice. beta, by contrast, IS sigmoid'd in the graph before the op (lines 365). ssm_a tensor already stores -exp(A_log) (the negative), so g_raw = softplus_out * ssm_a — sign already baked in.

L2 NORM ON Q,K (not RMSNorm): q_conv/k_conv get ggml_l2_norm (x/sqrt(sum(x^2)+eps)) along head_dim BEFORE the scan, eps=1e-6. The scan kernel itself does NOT normalize (unlike FLA's use_qk_l2norm_in_kernel=True). Q is NOT pre-scaled in the fused path — the kernel multiplies output by scale=1/sqrt(128) at the end (line 110); the chunking/AR ggml paths instead pre-scale q (ggml_scale line 47/321). Pick one, not both.

STATE LAYOUT IS TRANSPOSED/COLUMN-MAJOR in the kernel: state stored as M[col][i]=S[i][col], row 'col' contiguous (comment line 54). Input s0 layout [S_v,S_v,H,n_seqs] seq stride = H*S_v*S_v; per-(seq,head) offset h*S_v*S_v. Output state offset (seq*H+h)*S_v*S_v. Get this wrong and state carries garbage across decode steps.

CONV CHANNEL SPLIT ORDER: after conv+SiLU, the 8192 channels split as q[0:2048], k[2048:4096], v[4096:8192] with stride nb1_qkv=row_size(qkv_dim=8192). v offset = row_size(2*head_k_dim*num_k_heads)=2*2048. conv1d is depthwise (groups=channels), causal, kernel=4, and the prev-step conv window (3 cols) is prepended from conv_state cache.

GVA REPEAT: q,k have 16 heads, v has 32. Must repeat-interleave q,k by factor 2 to 32 heads (qwen3next.cpp 489-508 does reshape->repeat->reshape interleave, NOT a plain tile). The fused GDN op can avoid the repeat (handles GVA internally via iq1=fastmodulo(h_idx, neqk1) mapping v-head->k-head, lines 35/64), so if porting the fused kernel keep q/k at 16 heads and let the index math map; if doing it manually, repeat-interleave correctly (head h_v maps to h_k = h_v/(HV/H)).
CHUNK SIZE differs by gate type: CS=16 for KDA (Kimi), CS=64 for GDA (Qwen3.5). delta-net-base.cpp line 61. Use 64.

SCALE: 1/sqrt(S_v)=1/sqrt(128) NOT 1/sqrt(head_v_dim of value) — they're equal here (both 128) but the kernel uses S_v=v->ne[0]. The full-attention layers use 1/sqrt(256) (head_dim 256) — different from linear layers; don't share the constant.

ggml op output is a FUSED buffer: result tensor is [S_v*H, n_tokens*n_seqs + K*S_v*n_seqs] — the attention outputs and the K state snapshots are packed in ONE allocation; caller views them apart (delta-net-base.cpp 409-423, 577-602). For K=1 decode, state is the tail after attn_score_elems = S_v*H*n_tokens*n_seqs floats.

MUSA disabled, CUDA enabled: GGML_OP_GATED_DELTA_NET returns true on CUDA (ggml-cuda.cu 5433-5439) — so the reference CUDA path is live and can be diffed against; good for bit-level validation on the 5090.

Per-head g/beta are SCALAR (g->ne[0]==1), so the recurrence decays the whole 128x128 state by one scalar g_t — cheaper than KDA per-channel. Asserting g->ne[0]==1 guards against accidentally taking the KDA path.

components.component: Causal depthwise conv1d + rolling conv-state (the short conv that precedes the Gated-DeltaNet scan) for Qwen3.5/3.6 hybrid LINEAR-ATTN layers (il where (il+1)%4 != 0). VERIFIED on-device against the real Q8_0 9B GGUF, llama.cpp qwen35.cpp + delta-net-base.cpp + ssm-conv.cu, and the vLLM/FLA reference impls.
components.math:
SHAPES (verified from GGUF blk.0): ssm_d_inner=4096, ssm_n_group=num_k_heads=16, ssm_dt_rank=num_v_heads=32, ssm_d_state=head_k_dim=head_v_dim=128, ssm_d_conv=K=4.
key_dim = head_k_dim*num_k_heads = 128*16 = 2048. value_dim = head_v_dim*num_v_heads = 128*32 = 4096.
conv_dim = key_dim*2 + value_dim = 2048+2048+4096 = 8192 == d_inner + 2*n_group*d_state = 4096 + 2*16*128.

PIPELINE (qwen35.cpp build_layer_attn_linear lines 357-432):
1. mixed_qkv = wqkv @ x  -> [conv_dim=8192, n_tokens]  (attn_qkv.weight [4096,8192]). z gate = wqkv_gate @ x -> [value_dim=4096, n_tokens] (attn_gate.weight [4096,4096]); z is NOT conv'd — used later in gated RMSNorm.
2. conv: depthwise, per-channel c=0..8191, kernel taps w[c][0..3] from ssm_conv1d.weight (GGUF shape [4,8192], i.e. ne0=K=4 contiguous taps, ne1=8192 channels). NO bias in qwen35 (no ssm_conv1d.bias tensor).
   y[c,t] = SiLU( sum_{j=0..3} x[c, t-3+j] * w[c][j] )   (causal, left-pad with conv state)
   SiLU is a SEPARATE ggml_silu after ggml_ssm_conv (qwen35.cpp L394 then L397) — i.e. activation='silu'.
3. split conv output [8192, n_t] contiguously along channel dim into:
   q_conv = ch[0:2048]   -> view [head_k_dim=128, num_k_heads=16, n_t]
   k_conv = ch[2048:4096]-> view [128, 16, n_t]
   v_conv = ch[4096:8192]-> view [head_v_dim=128, num_v_heads=32, n_t]
   (order is Q|K|V; vLLM rearrange_mixed_qkv splits [key_dim, key_dim, value_dim]; the in_proj_qkvz interleaved layout is already de-interleaved into this packed Q|K|V before the conv — for GGUF/llama.cpp the weight is already in packed Q|K|V channel order.)
4. AFTER conv (not part of conv kernel): q_conv,k_conv get ggml_l2_norm (eps=1e-6) over head_dim; then enter the gated-delta scan. The conv kernel's job ends at step 3.

EXACT ggml_ssm_conv math (ggml-cpu/ops.cpp L9447-9456, CUDA ssm-conv.cu L40-56): for each (seq i3, token i2, channel i1):
  s = src0[i2 + i1*ncs + i3*..]  (sliding window of length ncs = K-1+n_t)
  sumf = sum_{i0=0..K-1} s[i0 + i1*ncs] * c[i0 + i1*K]   ; out[i1] = sumf
So conv input src0 = [ K-1 state cols | n_t new cols ] per channel, output dst = [d_inner=8192, n_t, n_s].

CONV-STATE CACHE (n_embd_r, llama-hparams.cpp L201-204): per sequence = (ssm_d_conv-1)*(ssm_d_inner+2*n_group*d_state) = 3*8192 = 24576 floats. Layout = [K-1=3, conv_dim=8192, n_seqs] = the last 3 input columns per channel (oldest first, newest last). The very first conv column is shifted out each step so only K-1 are kept.
components.chosen_design:
Two correct-first kernels, both plain f32 CUDA (NO tensor cores — this is a memory-bound depthwise conv, K=4):

(A) PREFILL / chunked-prefill kernel (n_t > 1): port llama.cpp ssm-conv.cu directly.
  - Assemble conv_input = concat([state [3,8192,n_s], qkv_mixed^T [n_t,8192,n_s]], dim0) -> [3+n_t, 8192, n_s] (delta-net-base.cpp build_conv_state L466-473: state reshaped to [K-1, conv_dim, n_s], qkv transposed, concat dim 0).
  - Run ggml_ssm_conv form: out[c,t] = SiLU(sum_j cinput[c, t+j]*w[c][j]); shapes out [8192, n_t, n_s].
  - Write the NEW state = last K-1=3 columns of conv_input back to the cache slot (build_conv_state L483-496: view of conv_input at offset (n_t) cols, ggml_cpy into conv_states_all at slot kv_head).
  - Use the two-variant launch from ssm-conv.cu: ssm_conv_f32 for n_t<=32 (registers, x[d_conv] ring buffer), ssm_conv_long_token_f32 for n_t>32 (shared-mem tiling, split_n_t=32). threads=128, blocks=(n_s, conv_dim/128, [n_t/32]). conv_dim=8192 is divisible by 128 (GGML_ASSERT nr%threads==0 holds).

(B) DECODE kernel (n_t == 1, the daily hot path): single-token shift-register update == vLLM causal_conv1d_update / FLA step. Plain torch-equivalent (FLA short_conv.py step comment, GROUND TRUTH):
    cache = roll(cache, -1, dim=-1); cache[:,:,-1] = x_new; y[c] = SiLU(sum_w cache[c,:]*w[c,:] (+bias=0))
  i.e. one block per sequence, 1 thread per channel (8192 channels -> 64 blocks of 128 threads, or grid (n_s, 8192/128)). Each thread: load 3 state taps + 1 new input, dot with 4 weights, SiLU, store y; then shift state (s0<-s1, s1<-s2, s2<-x_new) back to cache. This is the form to implement first because decode dominates.

components.sm120_kernel_plan:
STAGE 1 — CORRECT FIRST (decode, the daily hot path):
Plain f32 CUDA, no MMA. Kernel signature mirrors causal_conv1d_update.
  grid = (n_seqs, conv_dim/128), block = 128 threads. channel c = blockIdx.y*128 + threadIdx.x (c in [0,8192)).
  Registers: load w[0..3] = conv_w[c*4 + j] (GGUF ssm_conv1d kernel-major, ne0=4); load s[0..2] = conv_state[seq, c, 0..2]; load xin = mixed_qkv[seq, c].
  Compute: acc = s[0]*w[0]+s[1]*w[1]+s[2]*w[2]+xin*w[3]; y = acc/(1+exp(-acc)) (SiLU). Store y[seq,c].
  Shift state in place: conv_state[seq,c,0]=s[1]; [1]=s[2]; [2]=xin.
  State cache layout: [n_seqs, conv_dim, K-1=3] f32 (matches n_embd_r=24576/seq). One launch, fully memory-bound; ~24KB state + 32KB weight read per seq — trivially fast.
Validate bit-for-bit vs ggml_compute_forward_ssm_conv_f32 (CPU oracle) by feeding a [3+1]-col window.

STAGE 2 — CORRECT (prefill / chunk): port ssm_conv_f32 (n_t<=32, register ring-buffer) and ssm_conv_long_token_f32 (n_t>32, smem tile, split_n_t=32) from ssm-conv.cu verbatim with d_conv=4, apply_silu=true, bias=nullptr. Build the [K-1+n_t, conv_dim, n_s] input by concatenating the cached 3-col state with the transposed mixed_qkv (the ggml_transpose+ggml_concat in build_conv_state); after the conv, copy the last 3 input columns into the state cache. Drives chunked prefill of the 27B/9B.

STAGE 3 — FAST (optional fusion, later): (a) fold the wqkv matmul output directly into conv input layout to skip the explicit transpose+concat; (b) fuse SiLU + the subsequent Q/K split + L2-norm of q/k into one post-conv kernel (vLLM does fused_post_conv_prep). Conv itself is already bandwidth-bound — no MMA, no tcgen05/wgmma needed, so sm_120's lack of those does NOT matter here. Keep f32 accumulation (matches reference; conv weights stored F32 in GGUF even in Q8_0 models).

sm_120 NOTES: this kernel uses only generic FP32 FMA + shared mem — compiles cleanly to sm_120a, no special gencode beyond the standard -arch=compute_120a,code=sm_120a the project already uses. No PDL needed for correctness (the ggml_cuda_pdl_* calls in ssm-conv.cu are an optional launch-overlap optimization; drop them in stage 1).
pitfalls: CHANNEL ORDER: conv operates on the PACKED Q|K|V layout (q=ch[0:2048], k=ch[2048:4096], v=ch[4096:8192]), NOT the in_proj_qkvz interleaved-by-head layout. The GGUF attn_qkv.weight is already de-interleaved/packed; if you ever convert from HF in_proj_qkvz you must run fix_query_key_value_ordering (vLLM L653-701) FIRST. Splitting in the wrong order silently corrupts logits.
NO conv bias in qwen35 — there is no ssm_conv1d.bias tensor in the GGUF (unlike Mamba2). Use bias=0. The CUDA fuse_bias path requires bias to come WITH silu; here only silu applies.
Conv weight is kernel-major: GGUF shape [4, 8192] = ne0=4 taps (contiguous), ne1=8192 channels. w[c][j] = data[c*4 + j]. Do NOT read it as [8192,4].

State cache stores only K-1=3 columns per channel (oldest->newest), NOT K=4. FLA's ShortConvolution uses [N,D,W=4] (full kernel width) while llama.cpp/vLLM use W-1=3 because the first column shifts out. Pick ONE convention and be consistent between writeback and read; n_embd_r=24576=3*8192 proves the W-1 convention for this engine.

Causal alignment: output token t uses inputs [t-3 .. t]; the newest input x[t] multiplies the LAST tap w[3]. The ssm-conv.cu ring-buffer uses x[(i+j)%d_conv] indexing — verify your decode shift matches (s0,s1,s2,xin)·(w0,w1,w2,w3).
conv_dim=8192 is divisible by 128 (GGML_ASSERT(nr%threads==0)). If you ever change tile width, this assert can fire.

SiLU is f32 and applied to the conv output (pre-split). Q/K L2-norm and beta/alpha gating happen AFTER and are NOT part of this component — don't accidentally fold them in during stage-1 correctness checks.

Prefill state writeback order matters: write the new 3-col state to the cache slot BEFORE the next layer/step reads it; in batched/chunked prefill with n_rs_seq>0 there is a known TODO bug in llama.cpp (delta-net-base.cpp L498-500 [TAG_RECURRENT_ROLLBACK_SPLITS]) about last-(n_rs_seq+1) tokens crossing ubatch boundaries — for a single-sequence engine use the n_rs_seq==0 path (L479-496) which is correct.

Decode is the dominant path for the daily 27B/9B; implement and validate the single-token shift-register kernel FIRST, then prefill. Don't over-engineer the conv with tensor cores — it's pure bandwidth, K=4.

components.component: Full-attention layers (every 4th layer: il=3,7,11,15,19,23,27,31) + QK-RMSNorm + GQA(16:4, head_dim 256) + partial IMRoPE(64 dims) + per-head output gate + SDPA. Daily targets: qwen35 9B (32 blocks -> 8 full-attn) and 27B (64 blocks -> 16 full-attn). Same per-layer math, only n_layer differs.
components.math:
SHAPES (per full-attn layer, GGUF-verified from /home/avifenesh/ai-ml/models/qwen3.5-9b-judge-q8_0.gguf):
- n_embd=4096, n_head=16, n_head_kv=4, head_dim (n_embd_head_k=n_embd_head_v)=256, gqa_ratio=4.
- attn_q.weight = [4096, 8192]; 8192 = n_head(16) * head_dim(256) * 2. The *2 is a FUSED per-head (query|gate). Layout interleaved PER HEAD: head h occupies cols [h*512 .. h*512+512), first 256 = q_h, next 256 = gate_h.
- attn_k.weight=[4096,1024]=4*256, attn_v.weight=[4096,1024]=4*256, attn_output(wo)=[4096,4096]=(16*256, 4096).
- attn_q_norm.weight=[256], attn_k_norm.weight=[256] (per-head RMS over the 256 head_dim). RMS eps=1e-6 (9.999999974752427e-07).
- rope.dimension_count(n_rot)=64 -> PARTIAL RoPE: only first 64 of 256 head dims rotated, dims [64..255] copied through unchanged.
- rope.dimension_sections=[11,11,10,0] (sum=32=n_rot/2 pairs). rope_type=LLAMA_ROPE_TYPE_IMROPE (mode==40), NOT plain NEOX. freq_base=10000000.0, freq_scale=1, ext_factor=0 (no YaRN), attn_factor=1.
- kq_scale = 1/sqrt(head_dim) = 1/sqrt(256) = 0.0625 (f_attention_scale==0 -> default).

FORWARD MATH (matches llama.cpp qwen35.cpp build_layer_attn + vLLM Qwen3NextAttention._project_qkv_gate):
1. cur = RMSNorm(x_resid, attn_norm.weight)  [pre-attn norm, F32 weight]
2. Qcur_full = wq @ cur  -> [8192, n_tokens]. View as [256, 2, 16, n_tokens] stride; Qcur = even half (offset 0, stride 512 elems per head), gate = odd half (offset 256).
   In ggml terms (qwen35.cpp:272): Qcur = view_3d(Qcur_full, ne=[256,16,n_tok], nb1=elsz*512, nb2=elsz*512*16, offset=0); gate = same view at offset elsz*256.
3. Qcur = RMSNorm_perhead(Qcur, attn_q_norm.weight)  [normalize each 256-dim head]
4. Kcur = wk @ cur -> reshape [256,4,n_tok]; Kcur = RMSNorm_perhead(Kcur, attn_k_norm.weight)
5. Vcur = wv @ cur -> reshape [256,4,n_tok]  (NO norm on V)
6. Qcur = IMRoPE(Qcur, pos, n_rot=64, sections, freq_base=1e7); Kcur = IMRoPE(Kcur, ...)  [partial: first 64 dims; NEOX pairing x0=x[i], x1=x[i+32]]
7. attn = SDPA(Qcur,Kcur,Vcur, scale=1/sqrt(256), causal mask, GQA broadcast 4 q-heads per kv-head)  -> [256,16,n_tok]
8. gate_sigmoid = sigmoid(gate); attn = attn * gate_sigmoid  [elementwise, per (head,head_dim)]
9. out = wo @ reshape(attn,[4096,n_tok])  -> [4096,n_tok], added to residual.

IMRoPE collapse (KEY): for text-only batches llama-graph.cpp:146-156 sets pos[t]=pos[h]=pos[w]=token_pos and pos[4th]=0. The IMROPE kernel theta_base = pos[axis]*theta_scale^(i0/2); since all 3 axes equal token_pos and dim4 section is 0 (unused), the t/h/w section split is irrelevant -> IMRoPE == plain NEOX partial RoPE on first 64 dims. theta_scale = freq_base^(-2/n_rot) = 1e7^(-2/64). cos=cos(pos*theta_scale^j), sin=sin(...), for j=0..31; rotate pair (x[j], x[j+32]): out[j]=x[j]*cos - x[j+32]*sin; out[j+32]=x[j]*sin + x[j+32]*cos. Dims 64..255 unchanged.
components.chosen_design:
Two-kernel split staged correct-first then fast, all on sm_120a fatbin via cudarc:

DECODE (n_tokens==1, the daily hot path): port llama.cpp fattn-vec (D=256 instance). It is a plain-CUDA warp-per-KV-tile kernel using float accumulation + warp shuffles, NO tensor cores at all -> guaranteed to run on sm_120. This is exactly what llama.cpp's dispatch picks on Ada/Blackwell for Q->ne[1]==1 (fattn.cu:460-461). head_dim 256 IS a compiled vec instance (fattn.cu:258 FATTN_VEC_CASE(256,...)).

PREFILL (n_tokens>1): port llama.cpp fattn-mma-f16 (DKQ=DV=256 instance, fattn.cu:159-162). It uses ONLY mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.f16.f16.f16.f16 (mma.cuh:1001 / .f32 acc at :1163) which is Ampere-class PTX VERIFIED to run on sm_120 per the project spine (FP16 mma.sync runs; wgmma/tcgen05 do NOT, and this kernel uses neither). Alternative faster prefill reference: gau-nernst fa-5090 (https://github.com/gau-nernst/learn-cuda/tree/e83c256/07_attention) -- hand-written sm_120 mma.sync FA, but it is fixed to head_dim 128, so it needs adapting to D=256 (split into 2x128 K-tiles, single softmax) -- defer to fast phase.

CORRECT-FIRST FALLBACK (validate logits before any fused FA): implement the non-flash path from llama-graph.cpp:2131-2188 as 3 plain ops -> KQ = K^T Q (with GQA broadcast), softmax_ext(KQ, mask, scale=1/sqrt(256)), KQV = V @ softmax. Plus a separate per-head RMSNorm kernel (norm.cu rms_norm_f32) and the rope_multi kernel. Compare logits vs llama.cpp CPU before optimizing. QK-norm, RoPE, gate, o_proj stay as separate kernels in all phases; only SDPA gets the FA treatment.

components.sm120_kernel_plan:
STAGE 0 (correctness oracle, no FA): Implement plain CUDA kernels, validate logits == llama.cpp CPU on the 9B GGUF, ALL on sm_120a fatbin:
 (a) rms_norm kernel: 1 block per (row) , ncols loop, block-reduce sum(x^2), scale=rsqrt(mean+1e-6), out=scale*x*weight. Reuse for pre-attn norm (ncols=4096, nrows=n_tok) and per-head QK norm (ncols=256, nrows=n_head*n_tok / n_head_kv*n_tok). Port norm.cu:76-155 verbatim (drop the add/fastmodulo fusion).
 (b) qkv projection = cuBLAS/your existing matmul (wq/wk/wv are dense [4096,*]). Then a small view/split kernel to extract q (offset 0 stride 512) and gate (offset 256) per head.
 (c) rope kernel: port rope_multi (rope.cu:185-268) but you can HARD-SIMPLIFY to NEOX partial since text-only: for j in 0..31: theta=pos*powf(1e7,-2*j/64); rotate (x[j],x[j+32]); copy x[64..255]. One block per (head,token). ext_factor=0 so skip yarn ramp. Keep mrope-section code only if you want vision later.
 (d) SDPA naive: KQ=batched matmul with GQA broadcast (kv_head = q_head/4), scale 1/sqrt(256), apply causal mask (-inf upper triangle), row softmax (F32), KQV=softmax @ V. Two matmuls + one softmax kernel.
 (e) gate: out *= sigmoid(gate); then wo matmul.

STAGE 1 (decode fast): port fattn-vec.cuh D=256 instance (plain CUDA, 128 threads/block, no tensor cores -> compiles+runs on sm_120 trivially). Wire decode (n_tok==1) to it. This is the daily-token hot path.

STAGE 2 (prefill fast): port fattn-mma-f16.cuh DKQ=DV=256 using mma.cuh m16n8k16 f16 (Ampere PTX, sm_120-verified). Needs: ldmatrix loads of Q/K/V tiles to fp16 smem, mma.sync accumulate, online softmax. Cast K,V to f16 (kernel needs f16; build_attn_mha:2098-2104 does ggml_cast). If MMA tiling for D=256 is fiddly, alternatively adapt gau-nernst fa-5090 kernel (2x128 K-subtiles). Compile with -gencode arch=compute_120a,code=sm_120a.

All matmuls (wq/wk/wv/wo) reuse the existing block-FP8/MXFP4/FP16 mma path already proven at 762/381 TFLOPs.
pitfalls: RoPE is IMROPE (mode==40) per src/llama-model.cpp:2499-2501, NOT plain NEOX as the task brief stated. For TEXT-ONLY it provably collapses to NEOX partial RoPE (pos[t]=pos[h]=pos[w], llama-graph.cpp:146-156), so a NEOX kernel is logit-exact for text. If you ever feed multimodal/vision positions, you MUST implement the t/h/w/sections split (rope.cu:234-243) -- but daily judge/dense use is text-only.

PARTIAL RoPE: only first n_rot=64 of 256 head dims are rotated; dims [64..255] pass through UNCHANGED (rope.cu kernel :222-227). A full-256 rotation = wrong logits. theta_scale uses n_dims=64 not 256: powf(1e7, -2/64).

QK-norm weight already has +1 BAKED IN the GGUF (verified: blk.3.attn_q_norm values ~1.33 mean, not ~0.33). llama.cpp build_norm does plain rms*weight. vLLM adds +1 because it loads raw HF weights (GemmaRMSNorm). DO NOT add +1 when loading this GGUF or you double-count.

wq output [8192] is FUSED q|gate INTERLEAVED PER HEAD (stride 512 per head: [q_256 | gate_256]), NOT [all-q-4096 | all-gate-4096]. Extract via per-head stride 512, q at offset 0, gate at offset 256 (qwen35.cpp:272-296). Getting this layout wrong silently corrupts every head.

Output GATE is applied AFTER SDPA and BEFORE o_proj: attn * sigmoid(gate), gate is the raw (un-normed, un-roped) second half of wq output. Easy to forget -> this is Qwen3-Next's attn_output_gate, not a standard transformer.
QK-norm is applied to Q BEFORE rope and on the per-head 256-dim vector; K is reshaped to [256,4,n_tok] then normed then roped. V is NOT normed and NOT roped.

head_dim 256 is supported by BOTH llama.cpp fattn paths (mma case 256 fattn.cu:159, vec FATTN_VEC_CASE 256 fattn.cu:258) but the MMA prefill kernel requires K/V cast to F16 (build_attn_mha:2098-2104) and KV length % FATTN_KQ_STRIDE==0 padding (fattn.cu:454). Honor the padding or fall back to vec.

GQA broadcast is 4:1 (16 q-heads, 4 kv-heads); kv_head_index = q_head/4 (contiguous grouping). The fattn kernels exploit gqa_ratio for the ncols2 batch packing -- if you hand-roll naive SDPA, just index kv by q_head/4.
freq_base is 10,000,000 (1e7), not the usual 1e6/5e6. Wrong base = wrong positions for long context (262144 ctx).

MTP/NextN block (qwen35.cpp:488-644 graph_mtp) is a full-attn block with IDENTICAL attention math (same q|gate split, q/k norm, IMRoPE, gate) -- if you implement this component you also get the MTP attention for free; reuse the same kernels.
wgmma and tcgen05 do NOT run on sm_120 -- but neither fattn-vec nor fattn-mma-f16 use them (they use mma.sync m16n8k16, Ampere-class), so both are safe. Do NOT pull in CUTLASS/FA-3/FA-4 kernels.

components.component: Qwen3.5 hybrid forward orchestration + MTP/NextN spec-decode head + dual (KV + recurrent) cache + logit-match harness. Verified against the real GGUFs (9B judge f16, 9B NVFP4-MTP, 27B NVFP4-MTP main+mtp) with /home/avifenesh/projects/bw24/target/release/gguf-inspect and the ground-truth llama.cpp sources.
components.math:
FORWARD ORDER (per qwen35.cpp graph::graph, lines 136-228). embed = tok_embd[token]  -> for il in 0..n_layer (NOT n_layer_all; MTP block is loaded but NOT run): {
  inpSA = inpL
  x = RMSNorm(inpL, attn_norm[il], eps=1e-6)            // PRE-norm, applied to residual stream
  if is_recr(il):  y = LINEAR_ATTN(x, il)   else:  y = FULL_ATTN(x, il)
  cur = y + inpSA                                        // residual #1 wraps attn only
  ffn_residual = cur
  z = RMSNorm(cur, post_attention_norm[il])             // qwen35 uses POST-ATTENTION norm as the PRE-FFN norm (not a separate ffn_norm; tensor is "post_attention_norm")
  f = SwiGLU_FFN(z, il)                                  // dense: down(silu(gate(z)) * up(z)); MoE for qwen35moe
  cur = f + ffn_residual                                 // residual #2 wraps ffn only (added to pre-post-norm tensor)
  inpL = cur
}
h = RMSNorm(inpL, output_norm)        // === t_h_nextn (the MTP seed) captured BEFORE get_rows ===
h = get_rows(h, out_ids)              // select last-token rows (skip-if embeddings_nextn_masked)
logits = output @ h                   // lm_head [n_embd, n_vocab]; if output absent, tied to tok_embd.

So BOTH residuals are separate (attn-residual and ffn-residual), norms are: attn_norm = pre-attn (RMS), post_attention_norm = pre-FFN (RMS, NOT post), output_norm = final (RMS). All RMSNorm eps=1e-6, weights F32, no bias.

FULL_ATTN(x,il)  (build_layer_attn, qwen35.cpp 257-336). GQA 16 q-heads / 4 kv-heads, head_dim=256 (27B: 24 q / 4 kv). Qcur_full = wq @ x  -> shape [(head_dim*2)*n_head]=[8192] for 9B; FUSED query+gate: Qcur = view first head_dim of each 2*head_dim stride; gate = view second head_dim. Q = RMSNorm(Qcur, attn_q_norm[256]). K = RMSNorm(reshape(wk@x,[256,4,T]), attn_k_norm[256]). V = reshape(wv@x,[256,4,T]). Apply IMRoPE (mode=40) to Q,K with n_rot=64 (PARTIAL rotary: only first 64 of 256 dims rotated), sections=[11,11,10,0], freq_base=1e7. attn = softmax(Q·Kᵀ * (1/sqrt(256))) · V  (causal, GQA broadcast 4->16). attn = attn * sigmoid(gate). out = wo @ attn.   NOTE rope: IMRoPE interleaves 4 pos-channels (t,h,w,extra) per dim-pair sector%3; for TEXT-ONLY all 4 channels carry the same position, so it numerically equals NEOX partial-rotary over the first 64 dims. The brief's "RoPE NEOX" is functionally correct for text; the GGUF rope_type is IMROPE.

LINEAR_ATTN(x,il)  (build_layer_attn_linear, qwen35.cpp 338-470). Dims (9B): d_inner=4096, head_k_dim=head_v_dim=S=ssm_d_state=128, num_k_heads=ssm_n_group=16, num_v_heads=ssm_dt_rank=H_v=32, head_v_dim=d_inner/num_v_heads=4096/32=128. key_dim=128*16=2048, value_dim=128*32=4096, conv_channels = d_inner + 2*n_group*d_state = 4096 + 2*16*128 = 8192.
 (1) qkv_mixed = wqkv @ x  -> [8192,T] ; z = wqkv_gate @ x -> [4096,T] (the SiLU output gate).
 (2) beta = sigmoid(ssm_beta@x) reshape [1,32,T]; alpha = ssm_alpha@x reshape [32,T]; g = ssm_a * softplus(alpha + ssm_dt.bias) reshape [1,32,T].  ssm_a holds -exp(A_log) (pre-negated), so g = -exp(A_log)*softplus(...). dt_rank=32 (27B:48).
 (3) conv1d: prepend (d_conv-1)=3 cached cols, causal depthwise conv (ssm_conv1d [4,8192]) over the 8192-channel qkv_mixed, then SiLU -> conv_out [8192,T].
 (4) split conv_out into q_conv[128,16,T], k_conv[128,16,T] (the two key_dim halves), v_conv[128,32,T] (value_dim). L2-normalize q_conv,k_conv (eps=1e-6). Repeat q,k heads 16->32 (num_v_heads/num_k_heads=2) when not fused.
 (5) GDN recurrence per (head h, step t), state S[h] is [S_v,S_v]=[128,128] (stored transposed M[col][i]=S[i][col]):
       kv = Sᵀ·k                      (sum_i S[i][col]*k[i])
       delta = (v - exp(g)*kv) * beta
       S = exp(g)*S + k ⊗ delta
       o = (Sᵀ·q) * (1/sqrt(S_v))     scale=1/sqrt(128)
     (exactly llama.cpp gated_delta_net.cu !KDA branch lines 84-111; algebraically == FLA fused_recurrent_gated_delta_rule scalar-gate path.)
 (6) gated RMSNorm: out = RMSNorm(o, ssm_norm[128]) * silu(z)    (build_norm_gated)
 (7) cur = ssm_out @ reshape(out,[4096,T]).
Prefill (T>1) uses the chunked path (build_delta_net_chunking, CS=64 GDA) OR fused ggml_gated_delta_net; decode (T==1) uses autoregressive/fused. All paths must yield identical state.

MTP / NextN (graph_mtp, qwen35.cpp 488-644). One extra decoder block at il = n_layer (=32 for 9B, 64 for 27B); n_layer_nextn=1. Inputs: prev token id, and h = t_h_nextn (post-output_norm hidden of the main pass for the PREVIOUS position). Steps:
  tok = embed(token) via nextn.embed_tokens (or model.tok_embd if absent — 9B/27B GGUFs lack it, fall back).
  e = RMSNorm(tok, nextn.enorm); hn = RMSNorm(h, nextn.hnorm)
  concat = [e ; hn]            (dim0, -> 2*n_embd)
  cur = nextn.eh_proj @ concat   (eh_proj [2*n_embd, n_embd] -> back to n_embd)
  inpSA = cur
  then a FULL standard decoder block on cur: attn_norm -> full-attn (same wq/wk/wv/wo/q_norm/k_norm + IMRoPE + gate-sigmoid) + residual -> post_attention_norm -> FFN(dense for 9B/27B; MoE for qwen35moe) + residual
  cur = RMSNorm(cur, nextn.shared_head_norm or output_norm)
  cur = get_rows(cur, out_ids)
  logits_mtp = (nextn.shared_head_head or model.output) @ cur     (9B/27B: shared_head_head absent -> use model.output, tied lm_head)
This produces the +1 draft token's logits (speculative/multi-token-predict). Driven via LLM_GRAPH_TYPE_DECODER_MTP (llama-context LLAMA_CONTEXT_TYPE_MTP). The MTP block uses the SAME growing KV cache (it is a full-attn block) and writes one new KV entry.

components.sm120_kernel_plan:
STAGE A (CORRECT-FIRST, plain CUDA, no warp-mma, no tensor cores):
1. Orchestration in Rust/cudarc: run the per-layer loop on host, launch one kernel per op. Reuse existing proven sm_120a matmul fatbin for all wq/wk/wv/wo/wqkv/wqkv_gate/ssm_beta/ssm_alpha/ssm_out/ffn/lm_head GEMMs and for the MoE expert GEMMs.
2. RMSNorm kernel (F32 accumulate): one block per row, reduce sum(x^2), scale by w. Reused for attn_norm/post_attn_norm/q_norm/k_norm/ssm_norm/enorm/hnorm/output_norm. L2Norm = same minus the *w and minus 1/N (divide by sqrt(sum x^2 + eps)).
3. IMRoPE kernel: port rope.cu 205-265 but for TEXT-ONLY collapse the 4 pos-channels to one position array (all sectors read the same pos) -> equals NEOX partial rotary over first n_rot=64 dims; leave dims 64..256 untouched. Verify against llama.cpp with a single shared pos first; only add true 4-channel IMRoPE if multimodal positions are ever needed.
4. ssm_conv kernel: port ssm-conv.cu ssm_conv_f32 (short/decode) directly with d_conv=4 compile-time, fuse SiLU (apply_silu=true). For prefill port ssm_conv_long_token_f32. Read 3 cached cols from conv_state, write back newest 3 cols.
5. GDN scan kernel: port gated_delta_net.cu gated_delta_net_cuda<128,false,false> verbatim (S_v=128, scalar gate, no rollback). grid=(H=32, n_seqs, ceil(128/4)=32), block=(32 lanes, 4 warps). State stored transposed in the recurrent cache as M[col][i]. For prefill (T>1) FIRST just loop the SAME kernel over tokens with n_tokens=T inside the kernel (the kernel already loops t=0..n_tokens) — this is the simplest correct path and avoids the chunked solve_tri/cumsum machinery. Only after logits match, consider the chunked path for prefill speed.
6. gated output norm: RMSNorm(o)*silu(z) fused or two kernels.
7. SwiGLU FFN: two GEMMs (gate,up) + silu*mul + down GEMM. MoE: top-k softmax router (small kernel) + grouped expert GEMMs + shared expert (gated by sigmoid) — reuse matmul.
8. MTP: same kernels; just feed t_h_nextn (captured from the main pass output_norm) + the new token embedding through enorm/hnorm/concat/eh_proj then a normal full-attn+FFN block.
STAGE B (FAST): replace GEMMs with the sm_120a FP16/FP8/MXFP4 block-scale mma.sync paths already proven; keep GDN/conv/rope/norm as the small custom kernels (they are memory-bound, not MMA-bound). Optionally port the chunked GDN prefill (needs cumsum, tri, solve_tri, batched matmul) for long-prompt throughput. Do NOT pursue wgmma/tcgen05 (unsupported on sm_120). The GDN warp kernel uses only warp_reduce_sum + registers — fully sm_120 compatible.
STATE/CACHE: conv_state buffer [n_embd_r=24576, n_seqs] and ssm_state [524288, n_seqs] per linear layer, persistent across decode steps; KV cache [1024,n_ctx] x2 per full-attn layer (+MTP). For decode, T=1, so GDN+conv are pure register/elementwise; cheap.
pitfalls: RoPE is IMROPE (mode=40), NOT plain NEOX. For text-only it numerically reduces to NEOX partial-rotary (all 4 pos channels share one position), so implementing NEOX-over-first-64-dims is correct for text — but the GGUF declares IMROPE and the sector indexing differs; don't hardcode a generic NEOX that rotates all 256 dims. n_rot=64 means dims 64..255 are NOT rotated (partial rotary).

PARTIAL rotary: head_dim=256 but rope.dimension_count=64. Only the first 64 dims of Q/K get RoPE. rope_sections=[11,11,10,0] sum to 32 = n_rot/2 pairs. Rotating all 256 dims will silently corrupt logits.

Fused Q+gate projection: wq outputs (head_dim*2)*n_head=8192 (9B). First head_dim per head = query, second head_dim = the attention output gate. The gate is sigmoid-applied to the attention OUTPUT (cur = attn * sigmoid(gate)), NOT to Q. The view strides are 2*head_dim. Splitting wrong destroys both Q and gate.

ssm_a tensor (LLM_TENSOR_SSM_A_NOSCAN) already stores -exp(A_log) (NOT A_log). g = ssm_a * softplus(alpha + ssm_dt.bias). Do not re-apply -exp(). Comment in qwen35.cpp line 376: '-A_log.exp() * softplus'. FLA fuses this only when use_gate_in_kernel; here it is precomputed into the weight.

Two SEPARATE residuals: residual1 = attn_out + inpL (pre-attn input), residual2 = ffn_out + (attn_out+inpL). The pre-FFN norm (post_attention_norm) output is NOT added to the residual — the residual is the pre-norm tensor (ffn_residual saved before post_attention_norm). Misnaming post_attention_norm as a post-residual norm is wrong: it is the PRE-FFN norm.

qkv_mixed conv channel order is [q_key(2048) | k_key(2048) | v(4096)] = 8192 channels; conv1d is depthwise over all 8192; then q_conv/k_conv are the two 2048 halves (each [128,16]) and v_conv is [128,32]. L2-norm applies to q_conv,k_conv ONLY (not v). Then q,k heads repeated 16->32 to match v heads before the scan.

GDN state is stored TRANSPOSED in the cache: M[col][i] = S[i][col], row col contiguous (gated_delta_net.cu line 54). The scan reads/writes with this layout; reading it as row-major S[i][col] will transpose your state and break recurrence after step 1.

Recurrent state is FIXED-SIZE (524288 + 24576 floats per linear layer per seq) and does NOT grow with context. Only the 8 (9B) / 16 (27B) full-attn layers have a growing KV cache. Sizing the recurrent state per-token (Mamba-style scan output) wastes memory and breaks the long-ctx win.

Prefill vs decode must produce IDENTICAL final state. The chunked prefill (build_delta_net_chunking, solve_tri/cumsum/tri) is mathematically equal to the sequential scan but numerically delicate. For correctness-first, run the sequential AR scan for BOTH prefill and decode; only swap in the chunked kernel after logit-match passes.

MTP block embed_tokens and shared_head_head are ABSENT in the real 9B/27B GGUFs (TENSOR_NOT_REQUIRED). Must fall back to model.tok_embd for embedding and model.output (tied) for the LM head, exactly as qwen35.cpp lines 521/636. Asserting they exist will fail to load.

The MTP block is loaded into layers[n_layer] but MUST NOT be executed in the main forward loop (loop bound is n_layer, not n_layer_all). Running it inline would add a phantom 33rd/65th layer and corrupt output.

t_h_nextn (the MTP seed) is the output_norm hidden BEFORE get_rows/lm_head, captured for ALL positions in masked mode or selected rows otherwise. Feeding the post-lm_head logits or the pre-output_norm hidden into the MTP head gives wrong draft logits.
Attention scale = 1/sqrt(head_dim)=1/sqrt(256) (kq_scale), but GDN scale = 1/sqrt(S_v)=1/sqrt(128). Different. Also beta uses plain sigmoid (allow_neg_eigval=False) — do NOT use 2*sigmoid.

qwen35moe FFN: routed experts use SOFTMAX gating (LLAMA_EXPERT_GATING_FUNC_TYPE_SOFTMAX) with expert_weights_scale, PLUS a shared expert gated by a separate sigmoid scalar gate (ffn_gate_inp_shexp). The dense 9B/27B have NO experts (build_ffn dense). Don't apply MoE path to dense models (ffn_gate_inp==nullptr assert).

LOGIT-MATCH HARNESS: build llama.cpp, run `llama-cli -m <gguf> -p <prompt> -n 1 --no-warmup --temp 0 -ngl 99` and dump first-token logits, OR use llama-perplexity / a tiny eval that prints raw logits; better: add a debug dump of result_output for the same single ubatch. Compare argmax (must match exactly) and top-32 logits with tolerance ~1e-2 absolute / 1e-3 relative in fp16 (GDN/conv done in fp32 should be tighter). Pin: same quant GGUF, same prompt, batch=1, greedy, RoPE base 1e7, eps 1e-6, KV+state fp32 in your impl first. Mismatched argmax => check rope partial-rotary, gate split, ssm_a sign, state transpose first.
